$comment: Bunshin per-repository configuration. The driver (driver.md) and the three agent briefs (agents/{implement,verify,review}.md) read EVERY repo-specific value from this file; the markdown procedure itself stays generic. EDIT THIS FILE for your project: fill in the Trello board id and your project's install/gate/dev-server commands and the benign-console-error allowlist. The pnpm commands below are EXAMPLES — replace them with your toolchain (npm/yarn/cargo/etc). See the bunshin package README for a field-by-field guide. This is the ONLY file bunshin adds to your repo — the driver + agent briefs are served from the installed package.

project.$comment: Human-readable project name, used in launcher prose and reports.
project.name: {{PROJECT_NAME}}

board.$comment: The Trello board that IS the queue (accessed via the mcp__trello__* tools). Status is encoded by which list a card lives in. ALWAYS resolve the four list ids by NAME at runtime (get_lists with boardId) — never hardcode list ids, they change if the board is recreated. Call set_active_board with boardId once at startup so later calls default to it.

lists.$comment: Each logical column maps to one or more acceptable list NAMES (aliases). A value may be a single string OR an array of strings. Matching is case-insensitive and ignores spaces, hyphens and underscores, so a board column named 'TODO', 'To Do' or 'TO-DO' all match the 'To Do' alias. The first board list matching any alias wins. Add or replace these with whatever your board actually uses.

git.$comment: Worktree + branch conventions. The driver creates one isolated worktree per goal at <worktreeBaseDir>/<N>-<slug> on branch <branchPrefix><N>-<slug> cut from <baseBranch>, where N = the card's Trello idShort and slug = kebab-cased card name (~5 words).

commands.$comment: Per-project shell commands the gates run. REPLACE these for your toolchain. `gateChecks` runs in Gate 1 and again after the merge rebase. `devServer`/`agentStart` are used by Gate 2 (behavioral); if your project has no web UI to smoke-test, adapt the verify brief accordingly. For pnpm keep the no-build-scripts flag — see installNote.
commands.installNote: --ignore-scripts is REQUIRED for pnpm: a fresh worktree has no recorded build-script approval, so a plain `pnpm install` errors ERR_PNPM_IGNORED_BUILDS: esbuild (exit 1) and that failure then fires on EVERY later `pnpm <script>` (its deps-status check re-runs install), breaking build/test/dev. --ignore-scripts settles it cleanly with zero file churn. --frozen-lockfile additionally prevents lockfile churn. (Drop this note / flag if you don't use pnpm.)
commands.gateChecks: pnpm typecheck && pnpm build && pnpm test

verify.agentTagNote: A trailing token in a card name marking a feature that needs an extra background process (e.g. a local agent) booted for Gate 2 via commands.agentStart. Leave commands.agentStart empty and ignore this if your project has no such path.
verify.benignConsoleErrorsNote: Substrings of EXPECTED offline console noise (endpoints not running during the smoke test) that Gate 2 must IGNORE. Only fail on NEW errors introduced by the change. Add your project's expected offline endpoints here (e.g. an API or agent localhost URL).

neverCommit.$comment: Files the implement/verify agents must NEVER stage (install-time churn), and Gate 3 must BLOCK if they appear in the diff — unless changing dependencies is the literal point of the goal. Adjust to your package manager's lockfile.
